channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Static electricity is a mystery but invisible carbon may be key
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01012-x

A thin layer of carbon on the surface of oxide grains changes how they exchange static charge.
item.dc:title: Static electricity is a mystery but invisible carbon may be key
item.dc:creator: Shamini Bundell

item.title: Anthony Leggett obituary: physicist who brought quantum theory to the macro world
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01010-z

The polymath also trained as a philosopher and won a Nobel prize for his theory of superfluids.
item.dc:title: Anthony Leggett obituary: physicist who brought quantum theory to the macro world
item.dc:creator: Philip Stamp

item.title: Quantum simulations verified by experiments for the first time
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00959-1

Physicists cross-checked quantum computer predictions against experimental data about materials' properties.
item.dc:title: Quantum simulations verified by experiments for the first time
item.dc:creator: Davide Castelvecchi

item.title: Topological soliton frequency comb in nanophotonic lithium niobate
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10292-2

An integrated lithium niobate–semiconductor platform generates topological soliton frequency combs on-chip, enabling turn-key, dispersion-agnostic comb sources without high-Q resonators or complex stabilization.
item.dc:title: Topological soliton frequency comb in nanophotonic lithium niobate
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Englebert
Robert M. Gray
Luis Ledezma
Ryoto Sekine
Thomas Zacharias
Rithvik Ramesh
Benjamin K. Gutierrez
Pedro Parra-Rivas
Alireza Marandi

item.title: Structural basis of supercoiling-induced CRISPR–Cas9 off-target activity
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10255-7

Cas9 structures explain topology sensing and off-target activation.
item.dc:title: Structural basis of supercoiling-induced CRISPR–Cas9 off-target activity
item.dc:creator: Quentin M. Smith
Sylvia Whittle
Ricardo J. Aramayo
Daniel E. Rollins
Adam S. B. Jalal
Deborah I. Egharevba
Kyle L. Morris
Alice L. B. Pyne
David S. Rueda

item.title: Electrochemical corrosion accompanies dendrite growth in solid electrolytes
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10279-z

Operando birefringence microscopy measurements of the stresses around growing dendrites in solid electrolytes show that stresses decrease as current densities increase, revealing a linkage between electrochemical and mechanical stability that informs the design of solid-state batteries.
item.dc:title: Electrochemical corrosion accompanies dendrite growth in solid electrolytes
item.dc:creator: Cole D. Fincher
Colin Gilgenbach
Christian Roach
Rachel Osmundsen
Aubrey Penn
Michael D. Thouless
W. Craig Carter
Brian W. Sheldon
James M. LeBeau
Yet-Ming Chiang

item.title: Superluminal correlations in ensembles of optical phase singularities
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10209-z

Ultrafast electron imaging shows full phase-space dynamics of optical singularities, which can reach superluminal velocities before annihilation and break the particle-like analogy of topological defects.
item.dc:title: Superluminal correlations in ensembles of optical phase singularities
item.dc:creator: T. Bucher
A. Gorlach
A. Niedermayr
Q. Yan
H. Nahari
K. Wang
